summary: Iteration 35 — Master Brain end-to-end test. Found and FIXED a P0 frontend crash (AdminLayout ReferenceError 'section is not defined'). After fix, all 9 acceptance scenarios validated: RBAC nav visibility, RBAC negative (super_admin denied UI + 403 API), master-brain-denied panel, Master Brain page + Action Log tab, the full PREVIEW -> ask-for-reason -> APPLY action protocol on scratch customer 9990002222, and the audit log row with user_name + reason + before/after. Backend 11/11 RBAC + API gating tests pass.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED (was P0). Inserting the Master Brain hero NavLink had removed the `{SECTIONS.map((section) => {` opener, so the entire admin sidebar threw `ReferenceError: section is not defined`, crashed the layout, and the red 'Master Brain' nav item was never rendered for master admins. Visiting /admin/master-brain also showed a runtime-error overlay (NOT the access-denied panel) for super_admin. Restored the map() wrapper -> sidebar renders, nav-master-brain shows for master only, denied panel shows for super_admin.

integration_issues.flow: Master Brain -> Action Log tab refresh
integration_issues.issue: Minor: the Action Log table does not auto-refetch after a write action is applied in the chat; user must switch tabs (or hit it after a delay) to see the new row. API returns the row correctly (verified via /api/master-brain/action-log).

action_items: (Optional) When Master Brain applies a write tool successfully, auto-refresh the Action Log query (react-query invalidate) so the row appears without the user clicking the tab a second time.
Add a regression test (snapshot or render) for AdminLayout so a re-insertion of a hero NavLink can't silently drop the SECTIONS.map() wrapper again.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminLayout.jsx: the SECTIONS.map() opener was deleted when the Master Brain hero was inserted between the Fundle Brain hero and the section loop — a regression-test or eslint rule (no-undef on `section`) would have caught this immediately.
master_brain_routes.py looks clean — three-step protocol enforced server-side via tool schema + _need_reason gate, and audit row is written inside the tool itself (not relying on the LLM to log).
master_brain_tools.py: bulk operations correctly capped at INLINE_CAP=20000 and batched at 1000; points changes always write a ledger row — good.
test_credentials: masteradmin@fundle.io / Master@2026 ; superadmin@fundle.io / Fundle@2026 ; scratch customer mobile 9990002222
seed_data_creation: None. Existing seeded scratch customer 9990002222 used; its balance is now 300 (was 0) after the QA grant.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AdminLayout SECTIONS.map() fix is in. Master Brain write-flow on 9990002222 has already been exercised once; balance is now 300 with one audit row (reason 'QA test goodwill'). For future runs use a different reason string or the adjust_points tool to deduct, so the action log accumulates distinct entries.
rca of the issue: Root cause: when the Master Brain hero NavLink was added to AdminLayout.jsx between the Fundle Brain NavLink and the existing `SECTIONS.map((section) => { ... })` block, the opening `{SECTIONS.map((section) => {` line was accidentally removed. JSX still compiled because the body of the map was syntactically valid JS (an `if` + JSX `return`), but at runtime React encountered the bare identifier `section` and threw `ReferenceError: section is not defined`, which crashed the entire AdminLayout (and therefore the sidebar + the master-brain-denied panel). Reproduction: log into /crm/login as anyone, navigate to /admin — full-page Uncaught runtime error overlay. Fix: re-added `{SECTIONS.map((section) => {` immediately before `if (section.adminOnly ...)`. Verified by re-running the Playwright suite; nav-master-brain now renders only for is_master_admin users, master-brain-denied renders for super_admin, and the full preview/reason/apply chat flow works end-to-end with the audit row written correctly.
